originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: A New Helping Hand for Cameroon's President!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi amazing kids across Africa and beyond! Your friendly news reporter is here with some exciting news. We love sharing stories about our wonderful continent. Today, we have a very special update from a country called Cameroon. Cameroon is a beautiful country in Central Africa. It has mountains, beaches, and even jungles! Many different animals live there, like elephants and monkeys. People in Cameroon speak French and English, and many other languages too. It's a land full of vibrant colours and friendly smiles.

Meet President Biya of Cameroon

In Cameroon, there is a very important leader. His name is President Paul Biya. A President is like the main captain of a country. He helps make big decisions for all the people living there. Think of your school's head teacher, but for a whole country! President Biya has been the President of Cameroon for a very, very long time. Did you know? A President works hard to make sure children can go to school, that there are hospitals for when people get sick, and that roads are built for safe travel. It's a very big job!

A Very Long Time in Charge!

President Paul Biya is 93 years old. That's a super, super old age! Maybe your grandparents are old, but 93 is even older than most grandparents! Imagine all the stories he must have. He has been the President for 43 years. Can you imagine 43 years? That's longer than most of your parents have been alive! It's like being in charge since before many of us were even born. That's a lot of birthdays spent as President. He started being President in 1982. Think about how much the world has changed since then! There were no smartphones or internet like we have today.

What Does a President Do?

A President's job is very important. They have to think about the whole country. They make sure the country is safe. They also try to make life better for everyone. This means building good schools, making sure there's enough food, and helping businesses grow. It's a huge responsibility that needs a lot of energy. Because the President has such a big job, he needs help. It's like when you have a big group project at school. You can't do everything by yourself, can you? You need friends to help you!

Meet the New Helper: A Deputy!

Now for the big news! For the first time in his 43 years as President, Mr. Biya is going to get a special helper. This helper is called a "Deputy." A Deputy is like an assistant or a second-in-command. They work very closely with the President. They learn from the President and help with many important tasks. It's a bit like when you play a game and you have a team captain, but then you also have a co-captain who helps the main captain. The co-captain learns all the captain's moves and helps the team.

Why is Having a Deputy a Good Idea?

Having a Deputy is a very good idea, especially when someone has been doing a big job for a long time. The Deputy can help share the work. This makes sure that everything keeps running smoothly. It's like having two sets of hands to make sure all the jobs get done. A Deputy also learns all the important things about leading a country. They watch and listen very carefully. This way, they can be ready to help even more in the future. It's like having an apprentice who learns from a master. Did you know? Many big leaders around the world have deputies or vice-presidents. It helps them manage the huge responsibility of running a country.

What's Next for Cameroon?

This news is exciting for Cameroon. It means that President Biya will have someone to help him manage the big responsibilities. It also means that new ideas and energy can come into the leadership. Working together as a team is always a great way to achieve good things. People in Cameroon are waiting to see who this new Deputy will be. It will be someone who cares a lot about Cameroon and wants to help make it even better.

Let's Learn About Cameroon!

Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature" because it has so many different landscapes. From sandy deserts in the north to dense rainforests in the south, and even big mountains like Mount Cameroon! It is a truly diverse and beautiful place. The people of Cameroon are known for their lively music and colourful festivals. They love to celebrate and share their culture. So, this news about a new Deputy is a part of their ongoing journey to keep their country strong and happy.

Working Together is Great!

This story reminds us all how important teamwork is. Whether it's in your family, at school, or in a whole country, working together makes things better for everyone. When leaders have helpers, they can do an even better job. It means more people are thinking about how to make the country a happy and safe place for all the children, parents, and grandparents. So, let's cheer for Cameroon and its new plan to bring in a helping hand. It's a big step for a country that is full of amazing people and wonderful places. Keep an eye out for more happy news from our fantastic continent! Bye for now!
summary: President Paul Biya of Cameroon, who is very old, will get a special helper called a deputy to help him lead his country.
whyMatters: This is important because having a deputy means someone new will learn how to lead, making sure Cameroon stays strong and happy for a long, long time.

content: The fuel cost is nearly double what the government budgeted for putting pressure on stretched finances.

title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
content: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

originalTitle: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
title: Liverpool's FA Cup capitulation ramps up pressure on Slot
content: Nobody would have believed that 12 months on from Liverpool's title win, a fanbase so united behind its team and its manager would be, at best, apathetic as to whether Arne Slot remains in the job.

title: Haaland's 'old-school' lesson for Liverpool as FA Cup record falls
content: Manchester City have beaten Arsenal and taken apart Liverpool in their past two games and Erling Haaland's return to goalscoring form has laid down the gauntlet for the run-in.

title: Rohl won't watch title rivals as Rangers enjoy top spot
content: As Rangers move to the top of the Scottish Premiership for the first time this season, head coach Danny Rohl is "convinced" they can finish the job.
